source_request: Plan GitHub issue #4 in /Users/brian/dev/github/kontourai/kontour-console: Define Kontour emitter, sink, and plane contracts.

criteria.description: Docs define control-plane vs telemetry-plane data types and authority boundaries.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: explicit control-plane and telemetry-plane record category sections, shared correlation guidance, and non-authority wording for telemetry.

criteria.description: Docs define `KontourEmitter`, `LocalFileSink`, `CompositeSink`, future `HttpApiSink`, and future telemetry/Otel sink roles.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: role table or normative prose covering each named emitter/sink role without implementing network or telemetry sinks.

criteria.description: Docs define multi-output fanout with stable event/projection ids and independent per-sink results.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: same semantic record id across sinks and per-sink delivery result fields/outcomes.

criteria.description: Docs define local-first fallback and at-least-once/dedupe semantics.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: local file output remains first-class, future sink failures do not erase local output, retries may re-deliver, and consumers dedupe by stable record id.

criteria.description: Docs explicitly state no generic sink executes action descriptors, opens URLs, or mutates product state.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: security-sensitive prose covering action descriptors, URLs, endpoints, commands, backend fetching, and product-state mutation.

goal_fit.summary: Pending execution: issue #4 should deliver a durable docs/spec-only producer contract that preserves R1-R6 and AC1-AC5 without adding hosted API, network sink, emitter code, product integration, or UI.
